summary: Small ground/stage cleanup PR that replaced a large inline-assembly implementation of func_801C0A70 in ground.c with matching C, promoted InternalStageID names into stage.h, adjusted a ground.h prototype, fixed assert string handling around lbl_804D44F8, and made a grshrine.c vector initializer local to preserve matching/layout behavior. No PR body or review comments were available, so intent is inferred from the diff.
what_changed: Removed a local InternalStageID enum from grlib.c and added a broader InternalStageID enum to stage.h covering many stage IDs from CASTLE through OLDKONGO, with comments naming their corresponding stages.
Removed static const s32 lbl_803B7D80[] from ground.c and replaced its use with an inline local enabled_stages array inside func_801C0A70 using named enum constants where known.
Reimplemented static asm func_801C0A70 as C returning BOOL and taking a Point3d* pos, preserving the random stage/player-position logic and the fallback call to Stage_80224FDC.
Updated ground.h declaration for func_801C0A70 from an untyped static void declaration to static BOOL func_801C0A70(Vec3* pos).
Changed lbl_804D44F8 from an SDA address macro setup into a real char array initialized to "archive", and adjusted inline assembly assert references to use lbl_804D44F8 directly.
Changed func_801C247C from assert_line(0x7E1, archive) to an explicit NULL check calling __assert(__FILE__, 0x7E1, lbl_804D44F8), likely to match generated code and string/data placement.
In grshrine.c, removed a file-scope static const Vec3 initializer and instead declared Vec3 v = { 0.5f, 0.0f, 0.0f } inside a local block with unused padding retained.
smart_moves: Moved InternalStageID definitions into stage.h so multiple ground/stage translation units can share symbolic stage IDs instead of duplicating small local enums.
Used named stage constants in the newly C-decompiled func_801C0A70 while leaving comments for disabled, unused, or still-unknown stage IDs, preserving uncertainty without over-naming.
Kept an explicit local enabled_stages array inside func_801C0A70, matching the original assembly behavior where the table was copied/used on the stack rather than relying on a global const table.
Preserved likely stack/layout-sensitive artifacts in func_801C0A70, including an unused int array and explicit loop/index behavior, which is often necessary for matching compiler output.
Converted assert_line to an explicit __assert call with the exact string symbol lbl_804D44F8, making the emitted code and SDA-relative references more controllable.
Scoped the grshrine.c vector initializer locally instead of using a file-scope const, a common tactic when data placement or load/store behavior must match.
decomp_lessons: When replacing inline assembly with C, keep the original control-flow shape visible: random gate, enabled-stage membership test, player entity NULL check, position update, then fallback helper.
Stage ID enums are useful shared header material once more than one file needs them; avoid per-file enum fragments when they encode project-wide constants.
It is acceptable to leave numeric IDs with TODO or unknown comments when evidence is incomplete; do not assign speculative stage names just to make an enum look complete.
Matching may require local arrays and intentionally unused locals to reproduce stack frame size, copy loops, or compiler scheduling seen in the original assembly.
For assert matching, the source expression can matter less than the exact emitted __assert arguments and string symbol placement.
Moving a const aggregate from file scope to block scope can be a deliberate matching fix if the original code appears to construct or copy the value on the stack.
naming_conventions: Internal stage IDs use uppercase enum constants such as CASTLE, RCRUISE, BIGBLUE, ICEMTN, INISHIE1, INISHIE2, OLDPUPUPU, OLDYOSHI, and OLDKONGO.
Unknown or not-yet-understood data/function symbols retained lbl_*/func_* names, such as lbl_804D44F8, lbl_803B7DEC, func_8026BE84, Stage_80224FDC, and func_801C0A70.
The stage enum comments use human-readable stage names, e.g. RCRUISE is annotated as Rainbow Cruise and BIGBLUE as Big Blue.
The C rewrite used descriptive local names such as enabled_stages, player_slot, nstages, stage_id, and xoff while keeping the original function symbol name.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: A large inline asm function at 801C0A70 was replaced by C with matching-prone constructs: explicit BOOL returns, a local stage array, an index loop, and fallback branch behavior.
The local enabled_stages list preserves disabled/unknown gaps with comments: ICEMTN disabled, FLATZONE disabled, IDs 0x17 and 0x1A unknown/unused, and raw 36/37 left unnamed.
The C code keeps unused stack locals in func_801C0A70 and grshrine.c, suggesting stack layout preservation for matching.
SDA macro references for lbl_804D44F8 were replaced by a real char symbol and direct label references in remaining inline assembly.
The assert in func_801C247C was rewritten manually as an if (archive == NULL) __assert(...) rather than using assert_line, likely because the macro expansion did not match.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that rename func_801C0A70 or document its gameplay purpose.
Search for later completion of InternalStageID values 0x17, 0x1A, 36, and 37.
Check whether ground.h's Vec3* prototype and ground.c's Point3d* definition were later reconciled or typedef-confirmed.
Find subsequent PRs that remove remaining inline asm around lbl_801C0C2C, func_801C14D0, or func_801C36F4 in ground.c.
Search for other assert_line to explicit __assert conversions involving lbl_804D44F8 or the "archive" string.
